ddls:
CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration option */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration option */,
  "bits" INTEGER /* Bit position of the configuration in the system */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Access type (e.g., R/W for read/write) */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the configuration */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the configuration option does */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the configuration (e.g., boolean, integer, string) */,
  "min_value" INTEGER /* Minimum allowed value for the configuration */,
  "max_value" INTEGER /* Maximum allowed value for the configuration */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the configuration */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was created */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the configuration */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "modules" (
  "module_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hardware module */,
  "module_name" TEXT /* Name of the hardware module */,
  "module_type" TEXT /* Type of the module (e.g., PMU, WDT, RTC) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the hardware module */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Vendor of the hardware module */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the hardware module */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change event */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration that was changed */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Value before the change */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value after the change */,
  "changed_by" TEXT /* User who made the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_history_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, technician, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the user was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "modules_configurations" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping entry */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the module */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration option */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_modules_configurations_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_modules_configurations_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Level of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration related to the report */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "generated_by" TEXT /* User who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_reports_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the configuration */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when this version was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_versions_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the module */,
  "status_message" TEXT /* Current status message of the module */,
  "status_date" TEXT /* Date when the status was recorded */,
  "status_level" TEXT /* Level of the status (e.g., ok, warning, error) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_status_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, sms, in-app) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_trails" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., login, update configuration) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date when the action was performed */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trails_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_templates" (
  "template_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each template */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the module for which the template is created */,
  "template_name" TEXT /* Name of the template */,
  "template_description" TEXT /* Description of the template */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("template_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_templates_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id")
)
